mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 6% triggers a negative labor market signal that suppresses corporate earnings growth and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the unemployment rate does not rise above 6% within 3 months of the trigger, or if forward returns are positive in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts below 900k units triggers a negative demand signal that suppresses consumer spending and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 900k units in 2 months
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if housing starts do not decline below 900k units within 2 months of the trigger, or if forward returns are positive in at least 70% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in industrial production above 102 index points triggers a positive supply signal that boosts corporate earnings growth and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns in supply-boosting periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% triggers a negative monetary signal that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if the federal funds rate does not rise above 4.5% within 3 months of the trigger, or if forward returns are positive in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in real GDP above 16000 billion dollars triggers a positive demand signal that boosts consumer spending and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns in demand-boosting periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in payroll employment below 137k thousand persons triggers a negative labor market signal that suppresses corporate earnings growth and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if payroll employment does not decline below 137k thousand persons within 3 months of the trigger, or if forward returns are positive in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in real disposable income above 4339 billion dollars triggers a positive demand signal that boosts consumer spending and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns in demand-boosting periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
